evidence.q1: Sections numbered 1 through 16 sequentially; cross-reference example: "Assumed Liabilities" definition lists "Section 4", and Section 4 exists.
evidence.q2: "Assumed Liabilities" is defined once in Section 1 and used consistently in Sections 4 and 9; no conflicting definitions.
evidence.q3: No vague time/quantity phrasing; terms like "60 days", "35 days", "12 months", and "24 months" are precise commercial standards.
evidence.q4: No archaic legal diction (e.g., no "heretofore", "witnesseth") — prose is modern and plain English.
evidence.q5: Contradictory sentences: "nothing in this Agreement creates an admission of successor liability or assumed employment obligations" vs. "Buyer acknowledges it will be treated as Seller's successor employer for all transferred employees and will honor Seller's employment practices after closing."
evidence.q6: Register is consistent throughout; all sections use the same plain English style and consistent use of "will" (e.g., "Buyer will deposit", "Seller will sell").
